description: Ingot v1 — The Builder: full-stack app scaffolder with interactive CLI and web configurator at ingot.ink

userStories.title: Set up monorepo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the monorepo scaffolded so work can begin on packages independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json with workspaces configured for packages/cli, packages/core, and apps/web
packages/cli is an empty TypeScript package with a bin entry pointing to src/index.ts
packages/core is an empty TypeScript package
apps/web is a Next.js app (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind)
Shared tsconfig.json base at root; each package/app extends it
packages/core is importable from packages/cli and apps/web via workspace reference
Build succeeds from the root
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define the ingot.json Zod schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a validated schema for ingot.json so that all parts of the system share a single source of truth for valid build configs.
acceptanceCriteria: Zod schema defined in packages/core/src/schema.ts covering all v1 fields: name, framework, database.provider, database.orm, auth, ui.library, ui.theme, deployment
framework enum: 'nextjs' | 'tanstack-start' | 'remix'
database.provider enum: 'postgres' | 'mysql' | 'sqlite'
database.orm enum: 'drizzle' | 'prisma'
auth enum: 'clerk' | 'better-auth' | 'nextauth' | 'none'
ui.library enum: 'shadcn'
ui.theme enum: all 11 Shadcn themes (zinc, slate, stone, gray, neutral, red, rose, orange, blue, yellow, violet)
deployment enum: 'vercel' | 'cloudflare' | 'railway'
IngotConfig TypeScript type exported from packages/core
validateConfig(json: unknown): IngotConfig utility exported from packages/core
Invalid configs throw a descriptive Zod error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement package manager detection
userStories.description: As the CLI, I need to detect which package manager the user has available so all install commands use it consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/packageManager.ts exports detectPackageManager(): 'bun' | 'pnpm' | 'npm'
Detection checks for lockfiles first: bun.lockb → bun, pnpm-lock.yaml → pnpm, package-lock.json → npm
Falls back to checking which binaries are available in PATH
Falls back to npm if nothing is detected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement orchestration types and framework steps
userStories.description: As the CLI, I need the core orchestration types defined and framework scaffolding steps implemented so the system has a foundation to build other steps on.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/core/src/orchestrator.ts defines OrchestratorStep type with: label (string), command (string or function), envVars (string[]), optional postRun (function)
getSteps(config: IngotConfig): OrchestratorStep[] exported from packages/core/src/orchestrator.ts
Framework step for nextjs runs: npx create-next-app@latest <name> --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --no-git with all prompts suppressed
Framework step for tanstack-start runs the appropriate non-interactive init command
Framework step for remix runs the appropriate non-interactive init command
Framework step is always first in the returned steps array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement database/ORM orchestration steps
userStories.description: As the CLI, I need orchestration steps for all database provider and ORM combinations so those can be scaffolded after the framework.
acceptanceCriteria: Step defined for each ORM (drizzle, prisma) that installs the correct packages via the detected package manager
Drizzle step runs drizzle-kit init with the correct dialect flag for the selected DB provider
Prisma step runs prisma init with the correct datasource provider flag
Each DB/ORM step includes the correct envVars entries (e.g., DATABASE_URL)
DB/ORM step runs after the framework step in getSteps output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement auth orchestration steps
userStories.description: As the CLI, I need orchestration steps for all supported auth providers so auth is wired into the scaffolded project.
acceptanceCriteria: Step defined for clerk: installs @clerk/nextjs (or framework equivalent), writes middleware stub, adds NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY to envVars
Step defined for better-auth: installs better-auth, writes auth config stub, adds required envVars
Step defined for nextauth: installs next-auth, writes [...nextauth] route stub, adds NEXTAUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL to envVars
auth: 'none' produces no step (skipped entirely)
Each auth step includes a docUrl string pointing to the provider's official setup docs
Auth step runs after the DB/ORM step in getSteps output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement UI and .env.example orchestration steps
userStories.description: As the CLI, I need the Shadcn UI step and the final .env.example write step so the scaffold is fully complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Shadcn step runs: npx shadcn@latest init with the configured theme and --yes flag to suppress prompts
Shadcn step runs after the auth step in getSteps output
.env.example step is always last in getSteps output
.env.example step aggregates all envVars from all preceding steps and writes a .env.example file with each key on its own line with a one-line comment
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the interactive CLI
userStories.description: As a solo developer, I want to run npx ingot init and be guided through prompts to configure and scaffold my project.
acceptanceCriteria: npx ingot init launches a Clack-powered interactive prompt sequence
Prompts cover all fields in order: project name → framework → database provider → ORM → auth → UI theme → deployment target
Selected values are assembled into a valid IngotConfig and validated before execution begins
On confirmation, each orchestration step runs with a Clack spinner showing the step label
On success, prints a summary with project name, directory, and next steps
On failure, prints which step failed, the command that failed, and exits with code 1
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the non-interactive CLI mode
userStories.description: As an AI agent or power user, I want to run npx ingot init with a config file or inline flags to scaffold without prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: npx ingot init --config ingot.json reads and validates the config file then executes without prompts
npx ingot init my-app --framework nextjs --db postgres/drizzle --auth clerk --ui shadcn/zinc --deployment vercel works as a fully non-interactive one-liner
Invalid flag values produce a clear error message before any scaffolding begins
npx ingot init my-app pre-fills the project name and prompts for the remaining fields interactively
Execution after config resolution is identical to the interactive path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build the web configurator shell and layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ingot.ink app shell so the configurator and docs have a consistent layout to build within.
acceptanceCriteria: apps/web has a root layout with dark mode default, clean typography (GeistMono or similar monospace font)
Global styles match TanStack aesthetic: no gradients, no playful illustrations, typographic and structural
/ route renders a configurator page shell (header, main content area, sidebar or preview panel placeholder)
/docs route renders a docs page shell with sidebar nav placeholder
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build the web configurator step-by-step form
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to select my stack visually at ingot.ink through a step-by-step form so I can configure without memorising flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-step form covers all config fields in order: project name → framework → DB provider → ORM → auth → UI theme → deployment
Each step displays available options as selectable cards or buttons
Selected option is visually highlighted
A Next button advances to the next step; all required fields must be filled to advance
Step indicator shows current position in the sequence
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build the web configurator output panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the generated ingot.json and CLI command update live as I make selections so I can copy and run immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: Live preview panel shows syntax-highlighted ingot.json updating as selections change
Generated CLI command string updates in real time below the JSON preview
Copy command button copies the CLI command to clipboard and shows a brief confirmation
Download ingot.json button downloads the config as a file
Panel is visible alongside the form (not hidden behind a tab)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build the documentation site
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating Ingot, I want clear documentation at ingot.ink/docs so I understand what Ingot does and how to use it.
acceptanceCriteria: /docs route exists and renders a documentation layout with sidebar navigation
Docs cover: what Ingot is, quick start (CLI), full CLI reference, ingot.json schema reference, supported pieces table, FAQ
Quick start includes a copy-pasteable npx ingot init command
Supported pieces table lists all v1 enum values for each field
Pages are statically rendered (no client-side data fetching required)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
